claim: Agents are non-deterministic and non-robust: subtle prompt changes produce drastically different outputs, making them fundamentally harder to debug than traditional software
actionable: Accept that agents cannot be debugged like code; invest in trace-based observability and multi-turn context capture instead of stack traces
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Traces (full prompts, responses, tool calls, multi-turn context) are the source of truth for agents, not code—code only shows system prompt and tools, not actual behavior
actionable: Instrument every agent with comprehensive tracing; store full prompt history, responses, tool calls, and reasoning context for every run
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Agents have unbounded input space (infinite token combinations); traditional APM tools designed for predictable button-clicks fail to capture agent behavior complexity
actionable: Don't rely on traditional monitoring tools (Datadog CPU/RAM); build agent-specific observability for interaction patterns and decision trajectories
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Use online evaluators (LLM-as-judge) to scale human judgment; run them asynchronously on production traffic sampled or filtered by anomaly signals
actionable: Set up LLM-based evaluators for quality, safety, format compliance; run async on anomalous traces (low feedback, long latency) rather than all traffic
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Annotation queues with dedicated reviewers and rubrics enable human feedback at scale; route only anomalous traces for review rather than labeling all outputs
actionable: Create review pipelines for high-impact traces; define rubrics upfront; require multiple reviewers for critical decisions
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: LLM evaluators must be calibrated to your preferences; they don't automatically know what you want and require evals themselves to align expectations
actionable: Build a calibration dataset; run evals against your evals; iterate on judge prompts with ground truth feedback from domain experts
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: In-the-loop evaluators add latency; use offline evaluators for high-stakes decisions; reserve in-the-loop only when latency is acceptable and ROI justifies cost
actionable: Evaluate post-hoc by default; only embed evaluators in request flow if user latency tolerance and cost-benefit support it
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Focus evaluators on specific, narrow criteria (e.g., 'is user frustrated?') rather than broad rubrics; binary classifications work better and enable cheaper models
actionable: Design evaluators for single concerns (coherence, safety, format); use classification over ranking to reduce model cost and improve accuracy
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Production traces feed continuous improvement loops: capture → annotate → build datasets → run evals → update prompts/tools → re-deploy and monitor
actionable: Design your observability around closing the feedback loop; automate dataset generation from annotated traces and use evals to validate changes
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Trajectory evaluation (tool calls, decision paths) matters more than output-only testing; evaluate the reasoning process, not just final answers
actionable: Score agent decisions: how many tools were called, in what order, and why; penalize over-calling or inefficient paths even if final output is correct
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Agent observability requires cross-functional teams (engineers, PMs, domain experts, data scientists)—not just infra; align all stakeholders on evals and success metrics
actionable: Involve product, domain experts, and data scientists in eval design; don't leave observability to infra teams alone
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production

claim: Use Insights Agent to discover unknown agent usage patterns, error modes, and edge cases by clustering summaries of production traces at multiple levels
actionable: Periodically run clustering on your traces to find emergent patterns and failure modes you didn't anticipate during development
source_title: Deep Dive: How to Monitor AI Agents in Production
